{
  "mnemonics": ["Variable: A Name Tag for a Box (Think of it as a name tag you put on a box to remember what's inside. The assignment operator '=' is you putting the item INTO the box.)"],
  "keyPoints": ["Variables are named references that store data (values) in memory.", "The assignment statement uses the '=' operator to bind a value to a variable name (e.g., `x = 5`).", "Variables are dynamic; you can reassign them to hold different data types (e.g., `x = 5` then later `x = \"hello\"`)."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Using the assignment operator '=' when you mean to use the equality comparison operator '=='.", ["Attempting to use a variable before it has been assigned a value, resulting in a `NameError`.", "Assuming that assigning one variable to another (e.g., `b = a`) creates a permanent link; it only copies the value at that moment."]
}
